Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race

Blue Origin is the latest company to propose an orbital data center system, filing plans for a constellation of up to 51,600 satellites. The post Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race appeared first on SpaceNews.

Blue Origin's massive orbital data center constellation represents a new competitive frontier in space infrastructure that could reshape satellite communication economics for aviation.

Drones Mar 19, 2026

DJI’s smallest drone just hit it’s lowest price… again

DJI’s smallest drone has just gotten cheaper over on Amazon. The DJI Neo, DJI’s cheapest drone, is for a short time having a $50 coupon code available on Amazon, bringing the price down to just $150. This follows an earlier price drop last month. more…

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy on its entry-level Neo drone signals intensifying competition in the accessible consumer drone market segment.

Military Mar 19, 2026

Russia Sends MiG-31s Armed with Kinzhal Hypersonic Missiles Over Sea of Japan

Russian Aerospace Forces publicized for the first time the MiG-31 armed with the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal aeroballistic hypersonic missile flying in the Indo-Pacific region. Russia’s Ministry of Defense (RuMoD) released footage on Mar. 17, 2026, of its MiG-31I jets carrying the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic ...

Russia's deployment of hypersonic Kinzhal-armed MiG-31s in the Indo-Pacific signals escalating advanced weapons capabilities in contested airspace.

IFC systems must be designed with modular architecture to bridge the 30-year airframe lifespan against 5-year satellite technology evolution cycles.

Airframers embedding connectivity infrastructure at production reduces retrofit costs and accelerates IFC adoption across commercial fleets.

Lisa Anderson on shaping global aviation safety at the United Nations

UN aviation safety leadership requires bridging technical expertise with international diplomacy to establish harmonized global safety standards across diverse regulatory environments.

Military Mar 19, 2026

China’s J-16D EW Jet Seen Flying with PL-15 Missiles Possibly for the First Time

The J-16D has made possibly its first appearance in flight with PL-15 missiles, after being previously shown with air-to-air weapons only in static displays at airshows. The People’s Liberation Army Air Force’s (PLAAF) J-16D electronic warfare (EW) aircraft was spotted flying with two PL-15 air-t...

China's J-16D EW platform integration with advanced PL-15 missiles signals enhanced electronic warfare capabilities with beyond-visual-range combat potential.

USAF KC-135 center console modernization solicitation expected May, signaling investment in aging tanker fleet technology upgrades.

Industry Mar 20, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies is essential for balancing rising air traffic demand with environmental sustainability and airport capacity constraints.

See MDPI →

Press Release: British Airways’ first Starlink flight, a fitted 787, takes off

British Airways’ first aircraft equipped with Starlink Wi-Fi took to the skies today, the first flight operated by a UK airline featuring the innovative technology. The 787-8 aircraft bound for Houston is the first in the airline’s fleet to be connected, giving customers free access to fast, reli...

British Airways' Starlink-equipped 787 deployment signals a competitive shift toward satellite connectivity as a standard passenger amenity for long-haul operations.

Military Mar 19, 2026

U.S. F-35 Makes Emergency Landing After Allegedly Being Hit by Iranian Fire

CENTCOM says a U.S. F-35 made an emergency landing at a U.S. air base in the Middle East after it “was struck by what is believed to be Iranian fire,” according to sources quoted by CNN. A U.S. F-35 Lightning II fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at a U.S. air base […]

F-35 combat resilience demonstrated through successful emergency landing after hostile fire impact, validating aircraft survivability design.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate

A NASA laser reflecting technology that will aid Global Positioning System (GPS) accuracy is now operational as of March 9. The instrument, known as a laser retroreflector array, or LRA, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, the ninth of U.S. Space Force’s Block III Global Positioning System satellites,...

NASA's laser retroreflector technology on GPS III SV-09 enhances positioning accuracy, enabling more precise navigation for aviation operations worldwide.
